title: Sandalwood and cedarwood beard oil
subtitle: A lightweight beard and skin conditioning oil with sandalwood and cedarwood essential oils
excerpt: A beard oil is a light carrier oil blend applied to the beard and the skin beneath it to soften coarse facial hair and keep the skin from drying out. This blend uses jojoba and sweet almond oil as the base, scented with sandalwood and cedarwood. A few drops applied to a damp beard covers a full beard.
sourceNotes: Jojoba oil as a beard conditioning base: INCI Simmondsia chinensis (jojoba) seed oil. Sweet almond oil as carrier: INCI Prunus amygdalus dulcis oil. Essential oil blend for men's grooming: IFRA guidelines for leave-on skin products; sandalwood and cedarwood combined at 2% total. Beard oil formulation principles: Draelos and Thaman, Cosmetic Formulation of Skin Care Products (2006).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. A 30 ml bottle provides around 30 to 40 applications at 3 to 4 drops each.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable preparation. Make a full batch and store for up to 12 months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool dark place. Jojoba is the most oxidation-stable carrier oil in this blend and extends the shelf life. Discard if the oil smells rancid.

glossaryTerms.definition: A water-free preparation. Beard oil contains no water and requires no preservative.

content.text: leave-on product applied to the beard and the skin beneath. Its purpose is to soften coarse facial hair and prevent the skin under a beard from drying out and flaking. Jojoba oil is the preferred base for beard oil because it closely resembles the skin's own sebum and absorbs quickly without leaving a heavy residue. Sweet almond oil adds a light conditioning quality. Sandalwood and cedarwood essential oils together give a warm, woody fragrance that suits a leave-on application.
Measure all carrier oils into the dropper bottle. Add the essential oils. Cap and swirl to combine. Label with the date and contents. No heat is needed.

Apply after washing and towel-drying the beard. Dispense 3 to 4 drops into the palm, rub the hands together briefly, and work through the beard from root to tip, massaging into the skin with the fingertips. A little goes a long way; using too much leaves a greasy feel.
Apply a small amount to the inner forearm and wait 24 to 48 hours before applying to the face. This is standard practice for any new cosmetic preparation.
Store in a cool dark place. Keeps 12 months. Discard if the oil smells rancid or the colour changes significantly.
